name: AI Upscaler Plugin
description: AI-powered video upscaling plugin with Docker microservice architecture. Features automated hardware benchmarking, intelligent fallback system, pre-processing cache, and sidebar integration.
overview: Revolutionary AI upscaling plugin with Docker-based AI processing. Eliminates native DLL conflicts. Features comparison view, TV remote optimization, and sidebar integration.

versions.changelog: v1.8.3.3: Fix config-page Save (regression in v1.8.3.2). The v1.8.3.2 SSH cleanup left one stale, unguarded line in the settings Save handler (config.RemoteUser referencing the removed #RemoteUser input) that threw a TypeError, so saving plugin settings failed. Removed that line; no other change. Tests: xUnit 164, build clean.

v1.8.3.2: Remove dead SSH remote-transcoding (Issue #73). The docker7 image dropped sshd long ago (non-root appuser hardening), but the plugin still shipped SSH remote-transcoding config + UI, a /Upscaler/ssh/test endpoint that shelled out to ssh, and an FFmpeg-wrapper-script installer (/Upscaler/wrapper/*). All removed: 8 SSH config fields, the ssh/test + wrapper endpoints, FFmpegWrapperService + FFmpegWrapperController + the now-orphaned PlatformDetectionService, and the config-page sections. Offloading to a GPU host now uses the existing HTTP path only (set AI Service URL to http://host:5000, no SSH). Security: removes an admin endpoint that executed ssh and an on-disk script installer. Plugin-only; existing docker7 images unaffected. Tests: xUnit 164, build clean.

v1.8.3.1: Cancel-fix (plugin-only, no Docker change). jobs/{id}/cancel reported success but never stopped a running job - VideoProcessor.ProcessVideoAsync created a per-job CancellationTokenSource (the token CancelJob cancels) but passed the bare caller token to the processing pipeline instead of the job's linked cts.Token, so cancellation never reached extraction/upscaling. The semaphore wait, model-load and the method executor now all receive cts.Token (still linked to the caller token), so Cancel stops the job promptly. Pre-existing across all processing methods. Tests: xUnit 164, build clean.

v1.8.3: Opt-in pipeline parallelism (plugin-only, no Docker change). New experimental setting 'Pipeline parallelism' (default OFF) overlaps frame extraction with upscaling instead of running them strictly back-to-back: while ffmpeg is still extracting later frames, already-extracted frames are upscaled concurrently. Built on a thread-safe FrameStreamCoordinator that hands a frame to the upscaler only once a successor frame proves it was fully written, and drops the unproven highest frame if extraction fails, so the output frame-count and ordering are identical to the sequential path. Default behaviour is unchanged; the overlap runs only when the box is ticked. Works with the existing docker7 AI-service images (no rebuild needed). Tests: xUnit 164 (incl. FrameStreamCoordinator COMPLETE-vs-FAILED asymmetry + terminal-first-wins), dotnet build clean.

v1.8.2: Quality + hardening pass. (1) Denoise-before-encode prefilter (hqdn3d/nlmeans) - a dedicated source-clean pass run BEFORE upscaling/encoding, independent of the camera-style filters (cleaner input -> better SR + smaller re-encode). (2) VMAF quality scoring - admin-only POST /Upscaler/vmaf scores an upscaled/distorted file against a reference via ffmpeg+libvmaf (501 if the build lacks libvmaf). (3) Second frame-interpolation architecture beside RIFE: the engine is now input-signature-adaptive (RIFE 1/2-input, IFRNet 3-input, CAIN 2-input); IFRNet+CAIN added as experimental self-host. (4) MultiFrame extraction now reports live progress (previously only FrameByFrame/Batch). (5) Decoupled model download: POST /models/download-async + GET /models/download-status/{id} run big downloads in the background so they no longer trip client/proxy timeouts. (6) Supply-chain: all 6 Docker base images pinned by @sha256 digest + pip-audit CVE sweep (0 known). (7) New client-VSR-vs-server hardware doc chapter + verified FrameStreamCoordinator. Needs AI service image >= v1.8.2 (docker7) for /vmaf, /recommend and async download. Tests: pytest 73, xUnit 159, dotnet build clean.

v1.8.1: Live filter fix + hardware-aware model recommendation. (1) In-player live filters (presets/sliders) had no visible effect while realtime client-side upscaling (Anime4K/WebGL/WebGPU) was active - the upscaler renders to a canvas overlay that covered the filtered <video>, so the CSS filter sat on a hidden element; it is now applied to the visible canvas too. (2) New GET /recommend (service) and GET /Upscaler/recommend (plugin, admin) pick a model + scale the detected hardware can actually run (weak CPU -> fsrcnn-x2 @2x, dedicated GPU -> realesrgan-x4), avoiding the wrong-model-on-weak-hardware wall. The recommend endpoint needs the AI service image >= v1.8.1 (docker7). Tests: pytest 50, xUnit 137, dotnet build clean.

v1.8.0: Managed API tokens. A new 'API Tokens' tab (Jellyfin admin) mints named tokens for the AI service with an optional expiry (30 / 90 / 365 days or never); each is shown once, stored only as a SHA-256 hash, and revocable individually. The service keeps them in a persistent /app/config volume and validates X-Api-Token against the env API_TOKEN (now a non-revocable bootstrap) OR any active managed token - both timing-safe, expiry checked lazily (no background sweep). New endpoints: service /auth/tokens (GET/POST/DELETE), plugin /Upscaler/tokens (admin-only proxy). Requires the AI service image >= v1.8.0 (docker7) so /auth/tokens exists. Tests: pytest 22, build clean.

v1.7.13: Settings-page redesign + API-token guide (plugin-only, no Docker functional change). The plugin config page now adopts the Docker service's 'Operator Console' design - one consistent dark theme, cards, KPI tiles, chips, blue accent, Inter typography - as a CSS-only re-theme, so every setting and behaviour is unchanged (verified: field counts identical to v1.7.12). Plus a clear API-token setup guide on the website's API page and a sharper plugin field hint: API_TOKEN is a container environment variable you also paste into the plugin's 'AI Service API Token' field (or set API_TOKEN=disable on a trusted LAN); there is no 'generate' button. Tests 137/137.

v1.7.12: Timeout-wall + error-key fixes (plugin-only). First-time model loads that auto-download large models (face-restore GFPGAN/CodeFormer/GPEN ~280-377MB, large ONNX) no longer hit the 120s proxy wall: a dedicated 570s download client (just under the 600s UI wait so an over-long download surfaces a readable error, not a body-less browser timeout); /models/load UI timeout raised 180s->600s; benchmarks 120s->300s. The UI error parsers now read detail||error||message so the real cause is shown instead of a generic 'Load failed'. New DI test asserts the named-client timeouts (120/300/570) so a typo cannot silently fall back to the 100s default. Tests 137/137.

v1.7.11: Honest extraction progress + version-display guard. (1) The 'job stuck at 95%' fix now covers the frame-EXTRACTION phase - batch progress shows real extraction + upscale progress (frames done/total) instead of a time estimate that pinned at 95% during the long ffmpeg extraction on slow CPUs (#72); the dashboard panel shows the live phase (Extracting/Upscaling/Encoding) instead of 'Idle'. (2) Fixed the pipe-encode path that could also pin at 95% (unknown-total sentinel). (3) configurationpage.html now shows the real plugin version (v1.7.10 shipped still rendering v1.7.9), and a release guard asserts every user-visible version string matches the tag. Plugin-only (no Docker functional change). Tests 133/133.

v1.7.10: Fixes the recurring 'job stuck at 95%' + sharper GPU diagnostics. (1) Batch progress now reflects REAL frames processed (frames done/total + fps + ETA) instead of a time estimate that pinned at 95% on slow hardware; and a job with NO model loaded now FAILS FAST with a clear 'load a model first' message instead of grinding to 95% and hanging (#70/#72). (2) Setup Doctor /doctor now detects 'GPU + CUDA/ROCm available but inference on CPU' = a host driver/toolkit mismatch (right fix: update the host driver / check docker logs) instead of mislabeling it a CPU image (#71). (3) Plugin's displayed version (player/quick-menu/sidebar) synced to the real release. Docker AI service + plugin both at 1.7.10. Tests 123/123; /doctor pytests incl. the new GPU-inactive case.

v1.7.9: Setup Doctor + Anime4K embedded tier + GPU-state fix. NEW GET /doctor self-diagnostic (backend, GPU provider, device passthrough, onnxruntime build, API token, model smoke) plus a Setup Check panel in the Docker dashboard - condenses the #66/#69/#70 setup-friction saga into one curl. FIX: three AI-service endpoints (run_benchmark, /models/load, /benchmark-frame) reported the requested GPU intent instead of the active provider truth - now all use gpu_is_active(). The Anime4K anime-shader realtime tier now ships a vendored, tree-shaken Anime4K.js (npm 1.1.2, WebGL, MIT, ~225KB, Anime4K 4.0.1) embedded in the plugin DLL instead of a dead CDN load, with a WebGL2 float-texture support-gate and automatic Lanczos fallback; honest tier label (anime shader, not a neural net). Docker AI service and plugin both at 1.7.9.

v1.7.8: Docker AI service catalog + GPU/benchmark/AMD fixes. Model catalog 59 -> 71 (+12 ONNX models for compressed/streaming sources: realesr-general v3, RealWebPhoto/NomosWebPhoto, 1x DeJPEG/DeNoise pre-passes, Remacri, NMKD-Siax, HAT-L, RGT-S, fast low-VRAM picks). FIX (#70): run_benchmark() reads the ONNX session input shape so fixed-shape realesrgan-x4-256 no longer crashes warmup with a Reshape error. FIX (#69/#70): gpu_is_active() derives GPU-active from the live provider list (counts OpenVINO/CoreML) - /health,/status,/hardware,/gpu-verify stop reporting using_gpu false while OpenVINO is active. FIX: AMD image ran on CPU because plain onnxruntime shadowed onnxruntime-rocm - removed from requirements-amd.txt + force-install rocm as sole provider. FIX: VERSION reads APP_VERSION (was hardcoded 1.6.1.21). C# Plugin DLL: embedded model-fallback list refreshed 59 -> 71; functional fixes are all docker-side. Docker images: pull docker7-<backend> or :v1.7.8-<backend>. Tests 123/123. v1.7.x configs bit-compatible.

v1.7.7: Docker Self-Verification Hardening. Structural completion of the Intel-Arc saga (#45/#66/#67/#69). A dedicated docker-ai-service deep-analysis found no acute bugs left but the MISSING VERIFICATION LAYER that let the whole saga's bug class slip through: no Dockerfile checked at build time whether its expected ONNX provider was actually installed. That gap shipped the v1.7.5 Intel bug (plain onnxruntime built fine, no OpenVINO EP, ran on CPU while dashboard showed GPU). FIX: (1) Dockerfile (NVIDIA) + Dockerfile.intel now assert CUDAExecutionProvider / OpenVINOExecutionProvider after pip install - HARD-FAIL (exit 1) if missing, so a broken GPU image cannot be published. (2) Dockerfile.amd gets a warn-only assert (exit 0, since CPU is a valid AMD fallback when ROCm wheels are yanked) that makes the silent CPU fallback visible in the build log. (3) entrypoint.sh detect_backend() now knows WSL2 /dev/dxg so the startup banner no longer falsely says 'Backend: cpu' on WSL2 setups where main.py correctly detects the GPU. C# Plugin DLL bit-identical to v1.7.6 - all changes in docker-ai-service. Tests 123/123 unchanged. v1.7.x configs bit-compatible.

v1.7.6: Intel OpenVINO Provider Hotfix. Closes the last open Point of issue #69 (Intel Arc GPU detected but inference ran on CPU). Verified empirically via Laurent (FrRene06)'s /gpu-verify output: onnx_providers showed only [AzureExecutionProvider, CPUExecutionProvider] - no OpenVINOExecutionProvider at all. Root cause: requirements-intel.txt had a wrong comment claiming onnxruntime-openvino was deprecated and that plain onnxruntime + system OpenVINO would work; in reality, plain onnxruntime from PyPI does NOT bundle the OpenVINOExecutionProvider, even with system OpenVINO present in the base image. Fix: single-line swap onnxruntime -> onnxruntime-openvino in requirements-intel.txt. After Docker Hub rebuild + container restart, /gpu-verify shows OpenVINOExecutionProvider as active provider and Intel GPU is actually used for inference. C# Plugin DLL bit-identical to v1.7.5 - fix is docker-ai-service-only. Plus AMD build retry (Trivy timeout in v1.7.5 was transient). Tests 123/123 unchanged. v1.7.x configs bit-compatible.

v1.7.5: Non-Admin User Support + 4:3 Aspect-Ratio Fix. Issue #69 driven release. FIX #69 (Auth): audit found 47 of 52 endpoints in UpscalerController were RequiresElevation (admin-only) - including all playback/upscale/queue ops. Normal Jellyfin users could see models but not load or use them. Reclassified 31 endpoints to [Authorize] (authenticated-user OK) while keeping 16 admin-only (server-config, destructive, security-sensitive, global queue). Class-level [Authorize] on UpscalerController ensures all endpoints still require an authenticated session. Per-user GPU-quota deferred to v1.7.6. FIX #69 (Aspect-Ratio): both webgl-upscaler.js and webgpu-ai-realtime.js set canvas-overlay to 100% width/height without object-fit, stretching 4:3 source to 16:9 container. Added object-fit:contain - canvas drawbuffer already has correct aspect, so contain letterboxes correctly. Issue #45 closed as obsolete (same reporter as #66, indirectly fixed in v1.7.4). Tests 123/123 unchanged. v1.7.x configs bit-compatible. Repo now has 0 open issues.

v1.7.4: Docker-side fixes for two real-world bug reports. ISSUE #67 (eparrish64, 2026-05-19): NVIDIA users hit ONNXRuntimeError INVALID_ARGUMENT at warmup because _resolve_fp16_setting() auto-enables FP16 on Compute Capability >=7.0 GPUs but most catalog ONNX models are FP32-exported. New helper _session_input_is_fp16(session) checks the loaded model's input dtype before the cast in _onnx_infer_tile() and _onnx_infer_multiframe_tile() - FP16 only kicks in when both global flag AND model agree. ISSUE #66 (FrRene06, 2026-05-17): Windows 11 + Docker Desktop + WSL2 + Intel Arc A380 stuck on 'No GPU detected' because Intel-detection only searched /dev/dri/renderD*. New WSL2 detection branch via /dev/dxg + clinfo --list, new _parse_clinfo_intel_name() helper, new commented ai-upscaler-wsl2 section in docker-compose.yml with the verified Arc setup recipe. /gpu-verify endpoint exposes a new wsl2 diagnostics section (is_wsl2_environment, wsl_lib_mounted, ld_library_path). Plus 4 stale issues closed: #49 Vulkan (already supported docker7-vulkan), #64 Select Library (already implemented v1.6.1.14), #62+#63 v1.5.x install bugs (obsolete). C# Plugin DLL bit-identical to v1.7.3.1 - version bump + docker-side patches only. Tests 123/123 unchanged. v1.7.x configs bit-compatible. Repo now has 0 open issues.

v1.7.3.1: Hotfix + Interface-Extraction + Adapter Test Coverage. HOTFIX: external v1.7.3 audit caught that GET /Upscaler/js/{name} endpoint was announced as deleted in v1.7.3 release notes but a batch-edit interrupt left it intact - actually deleted now. PHASE D: 2 test seams extracted - IUpscalerCore (minimal interface, VideoFrameProcessor depends on it instead of concrete UpscalerCore) + IUserManagerAdapter (wraps Jellyfin IUserManager+IUserDataManager behind IsAnyUserPlayed(BaseItem), encapsulates fail-open). LibraryUpscaleScanTask now 1-line caller of the adapter. PHASE E: NEW UserManagerAdapterTests.cs with 2 regression-guards (null-item, fail-open-on-exception). POLISH: docs/MODEL-HOSTING.md annotation updated (v1.6.1.18 -> v1.7.3.1, registry size 48 -> 59). Tests 121->123. Build 0/0. v1.7.x configs bit-compatible. Deferred to v1.7.4: PlayCount/Played-flag tests (need Jellyfin.Data package ref) + VideoFrameProcessorTests + ProcessingMethodExecutorTests.

v1.7.3: Dead-Code Cleanup + Site-Doku-Sync + CI Drift-Prevention. Pure hygiene release - no functional changes. PHASE A: -540 LoC dead code removed (3 unused Service-Classes: TranscodingProfileManager, UpscalerTranscodingHelper-in-UpscalerTranscodingManager.cs, JellyfinConfigHelper + 1 dead DTO UpscalerSettings + 2 dead REST endpoints GET /Upscaler/js/{name} and POST /Upscaler/cache/config). PHASE B: Site-Doku-Sync - site/models.html extended with all 11 missing models (OmniSR/DAT-light/MAN/CRAFT/RestoreFormer++/GPEN/NAFNet), 14 site/*.html brand-version unified from {v1.6.1.17, v1.6.1.21, v1.7.0} to v1.7.3 via new Scripts/sync-site-topbar-versions.ps1. PHASE C: New verify-site-sync CI job catches future drift at PR time. CPUInfo + MemoryInfo kept (transitively reachable via BenchmarkResults). Tests 121/121 unchanged. Build 0/0. v1.7.x configs bit-compatible. Deferred to v1.7.4: Phase D+E (IUpscalerCore + IUserManagerAdapter interfaces + 3 mocked-async tests).

v1.7.2: DoS-Hardening + 6 Wishlist Models + Phase-2 Test Coverage Start. P0: 18 numeric properties get Math.Clamp upper-bounds (was Math.Max with no upper-cap) - closes DoS surface where Settings-Import could write arbitrarily large values. Plus 3 lower-bound drift fixes: RealtimeCaptureWidth (C#=64 vs UI=160), HealthCheckIntervalSeconds (C#=5 vs UI=10), CircuitBreakerResetSeconds (C#=1 vs UI=10). NEW: 6 wishlist models (MAN x2/x4, CRAFT x2/x4, GPEN-512, NAFNet-denoise). Catalog 53->59. NEW: ProcessingQueueTests.cs with 4 regression guards for the v1.7.0 debounce+async persist refactor. POLISH: ProcessingStatus.Analyzing enum value removed (was unreachable). CleanupOldEntriesAsync(ct=default) flows ct through to _cleanupLock.WaitAsync. Tests: 121/121. Build: 0/0. v1.6.x/v1.7.x configs bit-compatible. Deferred to v1.7.3: 3 of 4 Phase-2 mocked-async tests (LibraryUpscaleScanTask, VideoFrameProcessor, ProcessingMethodExecutor) - need IUpscalerCore + IUserManagerAdapter interface extraction first.

v1.7.1: WebGPU AI Realtime + 5 Wishlist Models + Cleanup Closure. NEW: 'AI WebGPU' realtime mode - real Real-ESRGAN compact inference in the browser via onnxruntime-web (Microsoft, MIT) + WebGPU compute backend. Defensive 4-stage fallback to Lanczos. NEW: 5 wishlist models (OmniSR x2/x4, DAT-light x2/x4, RestoreFormer++) - first catalog growth in 9 releases. Catalog: 48 -> 53 models. CLEANUP: RealtimeModeRegistry single source of truth (replaces inline allowlist), VideoFilterService.SupportedPresets (replaces _validFilterPresets array with 5 inline references), generic RegistryDriftLockTests [Theory] (replaces 3 separate HtmlDropdown_* methods, now covers 5 dropdowns). NEW CI: 3-job audit workflow (verify-fallback-sync, audit-tryapply-lambdas, zip-version-check) catches the drift bug classes that escaped 9 prior releases - including the v1.7.0 meta.json-in-ZIP catch. Polish: HttpUpscalerService LogDebug->LogWarning. Tests: 117/117. Build: 0/0. Saved v1.6.x/v1.7.0 configs are bit-compatible. Deferred to v1.7.2: Phase 2 mocked-async tests (need test-DI infra) + remaining polish (6 Clamp-drift, dead enum, _cleanupLock(ct)).

v1.7.0: Anime4K Realtime + Adoption Completion + Settings-Import Fix. NEW: real AI-shader realtime upscaling for anime via Anime4K.js library (MIT, jsdelivr CDN). HONESTY: existing 'WebGL' mode renamed 'Lanczos+Sharpen' (was always classical, never AI). RealtimeMode dropdown: 4 honest options - Auto / Lanczos+Sharpen / Anime4K / Server-AI. P0 BUG FIXES: (1) QualityLevel save-drift via QualityLevelRegistry. (2) ButtonPosition save-drift via ButtonPositionRegistry. (3) VideoFrameProcessor.cs:252 cancellationToken propagation closes the v20/v21 outer-loop adoption hole. (4) ProcessingMethodExecutor.cs:619-621 WaitForExitAsync(linkedCts.Token) replaces Task.Run().WaitForExit(60s). P1: 5 missing HttpContext.RequestAborted, UpscalerService.cs:119 IsServiceAvailableAsync(ct), ProcessingQueue PersistQueue debounced async (was sync inside lock). 18 missing TryApply lambdas added. +13 tests (102->115). dotnet build 0/0. WebGPU+ONNX deferred to v1.7.1. Saved v1.6.x configs bit-compatible.

v1.6.1.23: OutputCodec Save-Validation Fix (P0). Hotfix for a high-impact silent bug surfaced in the v22 deep-audit: the Settings #OutputCodec dropdown advertised 12 codecs (libx264, libx265, libsvtav1, libaom-av1, libvpx-vp9, h264_nvenc, hevc_nvenc, av1_nvenc, h264_qsv, hevc_qsv, av1_qsv, copy) but four code paths each had their own inline allowlist of different sizes. Worst case: UpscalerController Save endpoint accepted only 3 (libx264/libx265/copy), so picking AV1/NVENC/QSV in the dropdown silently fell back to libx264 - on NVIDIA RTX 40 hardware that is a 5-20x encoding-speed regression with NO error/toast/log shown to the user. New Services/CodecRegistry.cs is the single source of truth: OutputCodecs (all 12) for save+reconstruct+batch, RealtimeOutputCodecs (HW-encoders + libx264/265, no copy, no software AV1/VP9) for the realtime pipe path. All 4 sites (UpscalerController.cs:1437 save, VideoFrameProcessor.cs:400 reconstruct, ProcessingMethodExecutor.cs:477 realtime, ProcessingMethodExecutor.cs:803 batch) now reference CodecRegistry. New CodecRegistryTests.cs parses the embedded configurationpage.html at test time and asserts set-equality between #OutputCodec <option> values and CodecRegistry.OutputCodecs - drift-lock against future UI/registry desync. +17 tests (12 InlineData codec checks + 5 facts incl. HTML-parse). dotnet build 0/0, dotnet test 102/102 (was 85). Zero schema changes, zero new properties - v1.6.1.22 saved configs are bit-for-bit compatible.

v1.6.1.22: UI Honesty Cleanup. Removes 30 dead-backend config controls from Configuration/configurationpage.html that had no consumers anywhere in C#/JS/Python. Triple-pass verification over all 88 properties (v21 only enumerated 54 due to a regex that missed property-bodies like `{ get => _x; set => _x = Math.Clamp(...); }`). 5 entirely-dead <details> sections removed (Quality Metrics, Face Enhancement, Film Grain Management, Health & Circuit Breaker, Model Management). 13 dead toggles + 5 dead numeric inputs + 2 dead Face-Restore sliders + 3 orphan slider listeners removed from mixed-live sections. JS load/save arrays (fields, nums, floats, checks, sliderMap, longs) pruned in lockstep. Properties stay in PluginConfiguration.cs and Controller TryApply stays - saved user configs continue to load without crash. Deliberately preserved: ButtonPosition, EnableRealtimeUpscaling, RealtimeMode, RealtimeCaptureWidth (player-integration.js consumers), EnableFaceRestore + FaceRestoreModel (UI-direct-to-/face-restore/load REST consumers). Numbers: configurationpage.html 2807 -> 2680 lines (-127), <details> 14 -> 9. dotnet build 0/0 errors, dotnet test 85/85. Zero behavior change, zero new code paths - pure UI cleanup release.

v1.6.1.21: Adoption v2 + Compute-Waste Fixes + Honest Dead-Config. Closes all 8 findings of the v1.6.1.20 external audit. (1) WIRED long-dead RestrictToUnwatchedContent + SkipUpscaledOnRescan toggles in LibraryUpscaleScanTask via new IsAnyUserPlayed helper (DI extended with IUserManager + IUserDataManager). User compute-waste-protection finally honored. (2) FIX 6 remaining HttpClient calls now propagate HttpContext.RequestAborted - including hot-paths /upscale-frame and /upscale-video-chunk. 16/16 HttpClient calls in UpscalerController now use cancellation tokens. (3) FIX ProcessingStrategySelector substring-matcher tightened: removed broad 'compact' and 'realplksr' matchers that falsely accepted anime-compact-x4 (anime-category) and nomos2-realplksr-x4 (video-quality) for RealTime, causing frame drops. (4) FIX 4 sync File.Copy in frame-loops (VideoFrameProcessor + ProcessingMethodExecutor) replaced with async streaming. Pattern from v1.6.1.20 CacheManager:307 fix extended to all hot-paths. (5) FIX FaceRestore backend allowlist now derived from registry (category=face_restore) - symmetric to frontend dropdown auto-populate from v1.6.1.19. New face-restore models (e.g. RestoreFormer++) no longer rejected with HTTP 400 by backend. (6) FIX UpscalerController filter-preset list deduplicated 4x to 1x via new _validFilterPresets static readonly. (7) HONEST 6 unimplemented dead-config toggles (EnableModelPreloading, EnableHealthMonitoring, EnableModelAutoCleanup, EnableQualityMetrics, EnableFaceEnhancement, EnableGrainManagement) now flagged in XML-doc as 'currently no-op pending v1.7.0 pipeline implementation'. No silent UI-lying. NEW TEST FILE ProcessingStrategySelectorTests.cs +13 regression-guards. dotnet build 0/0 errors, dotnet test 85/85 (was 72). No model changes, no schema changes - v1.6.1.20 saved configs are bit-for-bit compatible.

v1.6.1.20: Adoption Completion + Cancellation + Async-IO. Follow-up patch closing the gaps the v1.6.1.19 post-release self-audit found. (1) FIX HardwareBenchmarkService.cs:123 - status.CurrentModel was bypassing EnsureModelAvailable via ?? null-coalescing; now wrapped. (2) FIX UpscalerCore.cs single-frame returns (7 sites) now route through PickAvailable for defensive symmetry with multi-frame paths (no behavior change today, regression-guard for future). (3) NEW BUG CLASS: 9x HttpClient GetAsync/PostAsync in UpscalerController got HttpContext.RequestAborted - prevents 120s hangs when client disconnects. (4) NEW BUG CLASS: CacheManager.cs:307 synchronous File.Copy replaced with async streaming via FileStream + CopyToAsync (useAsync:true) - was blocking thread-pool thread for 5-30s on NAS-mounted disks. (5) FIX csproj-Comments contained explicit v1.6.1.19 version-strings that pauschal version-bump regex would have falsely re-attributed; comments now timeless. (6) +7 new tests SingleFramePaths_AlwaysRouteThroughPickAvailable covering all 7 single-frame branches. dotnet build 0/0 errors, dotnet test 72/72 (was 65). No model changes, no schema changes, no breaking config - v1.6.1.19 saved configs are bit-for-bit compatible.

v1.6.1.19: Structural fix release closing the drift class that v1.6.1.17 and v1.6.1.18 patched point-by-point. (1) NEW Services/ModelAvailability.cs - single source of truth (HashSet + IsKnownUnavailable + PickAvailable) consumed by both UpscalerCore (refactored, kept logging-wrapper) and HardwareBenchmarkService (new EnsureModelAvailable helper hardens 7 previously-unguarded hardcoded model assignments). (2) FIX Face-Restore dropdown was hardcoded HTML <option>s for gfpgan-v1.4 / codeformer. Now auto-populated from /Upscaler/models category=face_restore — symmetric to the v1.6.1.17/18 main + anime/live-action dropdowns. (3) FIX Homepage card on site/index.html showed the v1.6.1.16 FFmpeg-fix text under a v1.6.1.18 header (pauschal version-bump bumped <h3> title without rewriting body); rewrote card with actual release content. (4) NEW <InternalsVisibleTo Include='JellyfinUpscalerPlugin.Tests' /> grant in csproj so tests directly assert on the internal ModelAvailability contract. (5) NEW JellyfinUpscalerPlugin.Tests/Services/ModelAvailabilityTests.cs +28 tests with explicit drift-locks (HaveCount(5), NotContain('realesrgan-x4')) + case-insensitivity + null/empty handling + PickAvailable semantics. dotnet build 0/0 errors, dotnet test 65/65 (was 37). No model changes, no schema changes, no breaking config — v1.6.1.18 saved configs are bit-for-bit compatible.

v1.6.1.18: Follow-up patch to v1.6.1.17 fixing 3 sibling bugs caught by external audit. (1) FIX PreferredLiveActionModel was Dead-Config (defined, UI dropdown, persisted, but never read by ResolveModelForVideo) — exact symmetric twin of the v1.6.1.17 PreferredAnimeModel fix that Verifier-A/B both missed. New non-anime override hook with PickAvailable fallback (override → ultrasharp-v2-x4 → nomos2-realplksr-x4 → realesrgan-x4). Setting Preferred Live-Action Model in Settings now actually does what the UI says. (2) FIX RealTime-AI rejected v1.6.1.17's bhi-realplksr-x4 (the model marketing literally calls Speed Champion) plus 4 compact-family models — Services/ProcessingStrategySelector hardcoded fastModels HashSet drifted from registry. Expanded 9 → 14 entries + safety-net substring matchers (compact, realplksr). All category=fast/video-fast models are now realtime-eligible. (3) FIX docs/MODEL-HOSTING.md still referenced 'v1.6.1.12 catalog'. Updated to v1.6.1.18 + new section recommending publicly-available drct-l-x4 over self-host nomos8k-hat-x4 and real-cugan-x4 over gated apisr-x3. (4) NEW TEST UpscalerCoreAutoModelTests.cs +1 [Theory] LiveAction_NonAnime_NeverReturnsKnownUnavailableModel locks down the live-action path symmetric to the anime drift-protection. Verification: dotnet build 0/0 errors, dotnet test 37/37 (was 33). No new models, no schema changes, no breaking config — v1.6.1.17 saved configs are bit-for-bit compatible.

v1.6.1.17: (1) FIX Auto-Mode broken for multi-frame VSR — ResolveModelForVideo() in UpscalerCore returned animesr-v2-x4 / realbasicvsr-x4 / edvr-m-x4 unconditionally, but all three are available:False (self-host required). New PickAvailable() helper now skips known-unavailable models and falls back to single-frame equivalents (realesrgan-animevideo-x4 / ultrasharp-v2-x4 / nomos2-realplksr-x4). EnableAutoModelSelection no longer 500s on multi-frame service configs. (2) FIX Controller fallback list went from hard-coded 12 models to embedded models-fallback.json with all 43 available models. UI no longer hides 24+ models when Docker service is briefly unreachable. (3) FIX site/models.html drift — listed 8 fictional models (waifu2x-cunet, hat-l-x4, swinir-l-x4, realesrnet-x4plus, ...) that never existed in the registry; now auto-generated from AVAILABLE_MODELS via Scripts/sync-fallback-models.ps1. (4) FIX Anime default model changed from "" (which fell through to self-host VSR in auto-mode) to anime-compact-x4 (5MB, available=true). (5) CLEANUP ModelManager.cs removed — was registered in DI but never consumed (dead code, header still claimed v1.5.5.4). (6) NEW MODELS — Real-CUGAN x2/x4 (Bilibili anime, mayhug/Real-CUGAN), DRCT-L x4 (Phips/aaronespasa, sharper than DAT2 on photo), BHI-RealPLKSR x4 (Phhofm, 2x throughput vs DAT2), RIFE v4.25 (yuvraj108c, current SOTA frame-interp). Catalog grew 43 → 48 models. (7) Docker service VERSION bumped 1.6.1.15 → 1.6.1.17 (was missed in v1.6.1.16 release).

v1.6.1.16: Hotfix for issue #64 — LibraryUpscaleScanTask was failing on every item with two related cold-start errors ("File not found: ffprobe" → then after partial fix "Cannot start process because a file name has not been provided"). Root cause: THREE singletons (VideoAnalyzer, VideoFrameProcessor, ProcessingMethodExecutor) all captured empty ffmpeg/ffprobe path strings at plugin construction time, before Jellyfin's MediaEncoder finished resolving /usr/lib/jellyfin-ffmpeg/{ffmpeg,ffprobe}. Fix: new EnsureFFmpegReady() in VideoProcessor re-resolves EncoderPath/ProbePath on demand, reconfigures FFMpegCore's GlobalFFOptions, and pushes the resolved paths into all three sub-services via new UpdateFFprobePath() / UpdateFFmpegPath() methods. Called at top of ProcessVideoAsync / ExtractSingleFrameAsync / ExtractSingleFrameWithFiltersAsync so scheduled tasks work even when the plugin boots before MediaEncoder. Verified live on Jellyfin 10.11.8 — scan task now extracts frames at 31% progress where previously it was 0/70 success. README header synced 1.6.1.14 → 1.6.1.16 (was missed in v1.6.1.15).

v1.6.1.15: In-player panel redesigned from AI-gradient/neon to a clean operator dashboard (Docker-Dashboard palette: #0b0d12/#11141b surfaces, #3b82f6 accent, underline tabs, monospace numerics). New live status strip at the top of the quick-menu: active-dot + state (ACTIVE/STANDBY/IDLE/DISABLED) + mode (Server/WebGL) + FPS + active model — polls every 500ms while menu is open so you can see whether upscaling is actually running. Docker AI service operator console redesigned (Grafana/Linear/Vercel): sticky topbar with live status dot + auth-posture chip, 4 tabs, KPI tiles, inline SVG latency sparkline from /metrics, model catalog with search+filter, hardware panel, API browser from /openapi.json + live Console tab that streams service logs via SSE. NEW Auto-Mode (default OFF under Settings → Auto Model Selection): when enabled, the plugin calls /Upscaler/recommend-model on playback start and picks both model and color-filter preset per video — anime → animesr-v2-x4/anime-compact-x4 + vivid, horror/thriller → drama, sci-fi → cyberpunk, documentary/low-res → sharp-hd, multi-frame batch → edvr-m-x4/realbasicvsr-x4. Override is session-only; saved preferences are preserved. Library-Scan codec picker expanded 3 → 12 options with per-codec encoder defaults. Player FPS HUD auto-hides in sync with Jellyfin's OSD fade.

v1.6.1.14: Select Library feature (issue #64) — scheduled library scan can now target specific libraries instead of always scanning everything. New chip-based library picker on Settings tab; new GET /Upscaler/libraries endpoint enumerates virtual folders; EnabledLibraryIds config field persists selection. Empty = legacy scan-all behavior. Bugfixes: saveConfig now actually writes EnabledLibraryIds (was missing from save payload, so selection silently reset every save); PluginVersion hardcoded string + header-meta + sidebar JS + csproj AssemblyVersion all synced 1.6.1.13→1.6.1.14 (previously save wrote old version back, plugin looked permanently one version behind). Docker AI service UI (localhost:5000): API_TOKEN-not-configured shows a single dismissible banner instead of repeat-spamming toasts; toast dedup 10s window + max-3 stack + click-to-dismiss; model search + status filter. FastAPI /status exposes api_token_configured/auth_enabled. Live-tested: all 26 config-page buttons across 6 tabs with 0 console errors.

v1.6.1.13: In-player quick-menu redesigned + live filter controls. The overlay that pops up from the Upscaler button during playback is now a tabbed interface [Models | Filters | Realtime] instead of a flat scroll. New Filters tab lets you tune the video look WITHOUT leaving playback: pick any of 15 preset looks (Cinematic, Vintage, Vivid, Sepia, Cyberpunk, Teal&Orange, ...) or drag 3 live sliders (Brightness / Contrast / Saturation) — the <video> element's CSS filter property updates at ~60fps for instant feedback, no re-transcode. An Advanced collapsible exposes server-side params (Gamma, Sharpness, Color Temperature, Vignette, Film Grain, Denoise) that persist via new POST /Upscaler/filter-config endpoint and kick in via FFmpeg on next seek. Reset/Save on both tiers. LIVE indicator dot pulses green while the CSS overlay is applied. Live sliders work fully offline — no AI service required; only the advanced FFmpeg params need the filter chain path.

v1.6.1.12: Bugfix + model-catalog cleanup. (1) gfpgan-v1.4 + codeformer URLs repointed from the never-published kuscheltier/jellyfin-vsr-models repo to the public facefusion/models-3.0.0 mirror — both flipped to available:true. (2) RIFE frame-interpolation replaced: old rife-v4.6 / rife-v4.6-lite pointed at a 404 v0.0.0 placeholder tag — now rife-v4.9 (quality), rife-v4.8 (balanced, new), rife-v4.7 (fast) from yuvraj108c/rife-onnx. MODEL_ALIASES map keeps saved configs referencing the old keys working transparently. (3) Five models without public ONNX mirrors (edvr-m-x4, realbasicvsr-x4, animesr-v2-x4, apisr-x3, nomos8k-hat-x4) now carry [self-host required] suffix + link to docs/MODEL-HOSTING.md. (4) New Scripts/verify-release.ps1 runs end-to-end post-release validation: SHA against live manifest, ZIP-contents scan for forbidden patterns (Moq/Mono.Cecil/CodeCoverage/.pdb/.deps.json) that caused the v1.6.1.11 wrong-ZIP-upload bug. (5) Docker service VERSION + Dockerfile LABELs + csproj/HTML/JS version strings synced. Net catalog: 32 models available (up from 30), 5 self-host documented (down from 12 broken).

v1.6.1.11: (1) Model-name regex widened to allow dots as separators (rife-v4.6, gfpgan-v1.4, rife-v4.6-lite no longer 400). Path traversal still blocked. (2) Docker catalog: 9 broken/pending models flipped to available:false so the UI filters them out instead of fast-failing. (3) Fixed 3 remaining raw fetch() calls in player-integration.js that were silently 401-ing (Upscaler/models/load, /upscale-frame, /benchmark-frame) — now use ApiClient.accessToken() bearer header. (4) GET /Upscaler/jobs now requires RequiresElevation — was leaking active-job file paths to any authenticated user. (5) Jellyfin.Controller SDK pin bumped 10.11.6 → 10.11.8 to match targetAbi. Bulk-load verified: 30/30 available models load.

v1.5.6.0: RequiresElevation on 8 admin endpoints, per-user rate limiting (10 req/min), Lanczos2 WebGL shader, Test Upscale button, Docker healthcheck, IPv4-mapped IPv6 SSRF fix, semaphore deadlock fix.
v1.5.5.4: 5 new configurable features (Quality Metrics PSNR/SSIM, Face Enhancement GFPGAN, Film Grain Management, Custom ONNX Model Upload, OpenAPI Docs) + dashboard UI + deep security hardening.
v1.5.5.0: Critical bug fixes — circuit breaker half-open bypass, ncnn CHW/HWC reshape, audio passthrough, max_concurrent=0 deadlock, health endpoint accuracy.
v1.5.4.4: Docker deep scan — 20+ functional bug fixes (thread safety, circuit breaker, tile blending, ncnn-Vulkan backend, per-model download locks). All 6 Dockerfiles verified.
v1.5.4.3: Fix bench buttons — auto-download models on load, forward Docker error codes, consistent API response format.
v1.5.4.2: Fix CORS errors in model catalog and performance monitor. Add live video benchmark.
v1.5.3.5: RTX 5000 Blackwell (CUDA 12.8) + Apple M5 CoreML support. 6 bugfixes.
v1.5.3.4: New AI models (SPAN, SwinIR, Real-ESRGAN x2+/AnimeVideo), output codec selection, ONNX tiling (OOM fix), 7 critical bugfixes.
v1.5.3.3: Real-time upscaling during playback — two-tier system (WebGL client-side + Server AI). Benchmark-driven tier selection, FPS overlay, auto-fallback.
v1.5.3.2: Fix model selection — correct model is now auto-downloaded, loaded, and used for upscaling.
v1.5.3.1: Scheduled task now pre-upscales videos, GPU fallback fixes (NVIDIA TensorRT skip, Intel OpenVINO diagnostics), improved /gpu-verify endpoint, player menu redesign.
v1.5.3.0: Fix player button injection (multi-path Docker support, config page bootstrap fallback), add Scheduled Task for library scanning, close stale issues.
v1.5.2.9: Fix FFmpeg wrapper 404 (dual route fix), fix benchmark 0.0s when Docker AI service unreachable, fix Test Connection cache, prominent Player Integration section. Fixes #48.
v1.5.2.8: Fix API route mismatch (503 errors on all buttons), config page rebuild (Jellyfin 10.11+), horizontal tabs + collapsible sections, 15+ bug fixes, dark theme. Fixes #39 #36 #48.
v1.5.2.7: Docker fixes (TensorRT/CUDA fallback, Intel OpenVINO GPU, multi-GPU selection), premium sidebar dashboard redesign, player button MutationObserver, health caching, retry logic.
v1.5.2.6: Fixed notification spam, forced English UI, dark theme input fields, XSS security fixes, settings export/import visible, checkbox fix.
v1.5.2.4: Major update - 30+ bug fixes, security hardening, dashboard redesign with tabs, fixed JS components, correct model names, thread safety fixes.
v1.5.2.3: Fixed NotSupported plugin status — lowered targetAbi to 10.11.0.0 for broad Jellyfin 10.11.x compatibility.
v1.5.2.2: Fixed player button not showing (Issue #45), Intel OpenVINO GPU fix.
v1.5.1.1: SSH configuration hotfix. Fixed SSH fields not saving/loading. Added Test SSH Connection button and backend endpoint.
v1.5.1.0: Real Remote Transcoding (SSH). Implements rffmpeg-style architecture with SSH tunneling, path mapping, and shared transcoding folder support.
v1.5.0.1: Hotfix - Fixed DI error (#34), Checksum fix (#33), Intel GPU/OpenVINO support (#32)
v1.5.0.0: Docker Microservice Architecture - Eliminates native DLL conflicts. AI processing via external Docker container.
v1.4.9.4: Final Release - Settings Page Fix, Cross-Platform Support, Complete DI Registration.
v1.4.9.3: Verified Service Registration and Settings Polish.
v1.4.9.2: Critical Update. Implemented Real AI Upscaling with ONNX Runtime, Fixed Dependency Injection crashes, Added Model Manager, and Hardware Benchmarking.
v1.4.9.1: Fixed sidebar integration and settings access. Dashboard now appears correctly in sidebar with working settings link.
